headline: Dalfen Industrial Accelerates Southeast Expansion with Fort Lauderdale Portfolio Acquisition
summary: Broward County, FL Dalfen Industrial Accelerates Southeast Expansion with Fort Lauderdale Portfolio Acquisition DALLAS, June 10, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Dalfen Industrial has acquired a nine-building industrial portfolio totaling 419,253 square feet in Broward County, Florida, further strengthening the company’s presence in one of the nation’s most sought-after infill logistics markets. headline: FedEx Reshapes Freight And Asia Pacific Strategy As Valuation Gap Persists
summary: FedEx has completed the spin-off of its FedEx Freight business into an independent public company. The company has signed an air logistics partnership with China Southern Airlines focused on the Asia Pacific region. These moves reshape FedEx's corporate structure and its approach to international air network capacity. FedEx, traded as NYSE:FDX, is making structural changes at a time when its stock price stands at $331.76. The company has posted a return of 13.2% year to date and 84.7% over...

headline: Amazon Supply Chain Services Announces U.S. Expansion Of Less-Than-Truckload Freight To Any Type Of Destination, Including Third-Party Warehouses, Distribution Centers And Retail Partners
summary: Businesses now have the flexibility to ship by pallet, choosing LTL to share trailer space for partial loads instead of reserving and paying for a full truckload. headline: The Average American Has Worked for 6 Employers and 23% Still Have Multiple Old Retirement Accounts, Fidelity’s 2026 Study Finds
summary: The typical American worker now passes through six employers over a career, and each job change leaves behind a retirement account to keep track of. Fidelity’s 2026 study found that 23% of Americans with retirement accounts still have multiple balances sitting in old or current workplace plans. That fragmentation is the quiet drag on retirement ... The Average American Has Worked for 6 Employers and 23% Still Have Multiple Old Retirement Accounts, Fidelity’s 2026 Study Finds
